summary: AIR-DROW Final Release Readiness & Cache Integrity Edition: release metadata, cache-busting asset revisions and final Android validation contracts are synchronized while physical actions, source-first exports and private on-device hand drawing remain protected.
features: Synchronizes the page build, module build, PWA manifest, service worker, release descriptor and cache-busting asset URLs under one v8.0.0 revision.
Keeps export, download, backup, diagnostics download and destructive clear actions behind trusted physical control activation only.
Preserves source-first raster export and optional quiet hand guidance so live UI overlays never enter saved artwork.
Adds final-release contract checks that reject stale cache markers and misleading hand shortcut documentation.
Includes a bilingual Android device-validation checklist for the camera, hand detection, palette, export, PWA update and offline hand-runtime checks.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you choose to export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: App readiness, hand drawing checks and local diagnostics read only browser/app state. They do not open the camera, upload a report, or include camera frames, hand landmarks, drawing points, project titles, or API URLs.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and hand model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The complete source package includes the verified hand model. The production build validates the local model before publishing.
